This family is part of the order Gentianales, which also includes other flowering plant families such as Apocynaceae and Loganiaceae. intro: Macrolenes macrosepala, also known as Kava Kava, is a plant species native to the Pacific Island region, belonging to the family Piperaceae. The roots of Macrolenes macrosepala have been traditionally used for their medicinal and recreational properties, providing relief from anxiety, insomnia, and pain, as well as promoting relaxation and reducing muscle spasms. The bioactive constituents responsible for these effects include flavokavains, kavalactones, and pipernonaline, which have been shown to possess anxiolytic, sedative, and anti-inflammatory properties. Traditionally, the roots of Macrolenes macrosepala are used to prepare a medicinal tea, tincture, or dried root powder, which can be used to support mental well-being and reduce stress.
